mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in months with PAYEMS growth > 100k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in GDPC1 growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rises in CPIAUCSL indicate stagflation risks, causing equity market volatility and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in stagflation scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes (UNRATE > 5.5%), rising consumer spending (RSAFS) signals economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in UNRATE > 5.5% periods with RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) reflect strong demand across sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST and INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
